speaker: KING
text: Lords, give us leave; the Prince of Wales and I

speaker: KING
text: Must have some private conference, but be near at hand,

speaker: KING
text: For we shall presently have need of you.

speaker: KING
text: I know not whether God will have it so

speaker: KING
text: For some displeasing service I have done,

speaker: KING
text: That, in His secret doom, out of my blood

speaker: KING
text: He’ll breed revengement and a scourge for me.

speaker: KING
text: But thou dost in thy passages of life

speaker: KING
text: Make me believe that thou art only marked

speaker: KING
text: For the hot vengeance and the rod of heaven

speaker: KING
text: To punish my mistreadings. Tell me else,

speaker: KING
text: Could such inordinate and low desires,

speaker: KING
text: Such poor, such bare, such lewd, such mean attempts,

speaker: KING
text: Such barren pleasures, rude society

speaker: KING
text: As thou art matched withal, and grafted to,

speaker: KING
text: Accompany the greatness of thy blood,

speaker: KING
text: And hold their level with thy princely heart?

speaker: PRINCE
text: So please your Majesty, I would I could

speaker: PRINCE
text: Quit all offenses with as clear excuse

speaker: PRINCE
text: As well as I am doubtless I can purge

speaker: PRINCE
text: Myself of many I am charged withal.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Yet such extenuation let me beg

speaker: PRINCE
text: As, in reproof of many tales devised,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Which oft the ear of greatness needs must hear,

speaker: PRINCE
text: By smiling pickthanks and base newsmongers,

speaker: PRINCE
text: I may for some things true, wherein my youth

speaker: PRINCE
text: Hath faulty wandered and irregular,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Find pardon on my true submission.

speaker: KING
text: God pardon thee. Yet let me wonder, Harry,

speaker: KING
text: At thy affections, which do hold a wing

speaker: KING
text: Quite from the flight of all thy ancestors.

speaker: KING
text: Thy place in council thou hast rudely lost,

speaker: KING
text: Which by thy younger brother is supplied,

speaker: KING
text: And art almost an alien to the hearts

speaker: KING
text: Of all the court and princes of my blood.

speaker: KING
text: The hope and expectation of thy time

speaker: KING
text: Is ruined, and the soul of every man

speaker: KING
text: Prophetically do forethink thy fall.

speaker: KING
text: Had I so lavish of my presence been,

speaker: KING
text: So common-hackneyed in the eyes of men,

speaker: KING
text: So stale and cheap to vulgar company,

speaker: KING
text: Opinion, that did help me to the crown,

speaker: KING
text: Had still kept loyal to possession

speaker: KING
text: And left me in reputeless banishment,

speaker: KING
text: A fellow of no mark nor likelihood.

speaker: KING
text: By being seldom seen, I could not stir

speaker: KING
text: But like a comet I was wondered at,

speaker: KING
text: That men would tell their children “This is he.”

speaker: KING
text: Others would say “Where? Which is Bolingbroke?”

speaker: KING
text: And then I stole all courtesy from heaven,

speaker: KING
text: And dressed myself in such humility

speaker: KING
text: That I did pluck allegiance from men’s hearts,

speaker: KING
text: Loud shouts and salutations from their mouths,

speaker: KING
text: Even in the presence of the crownèd king.

speaker: KING
text: Thus did I keep my person fresh and new,

speaker: KING
text: My presence, like a robe pontifical,

speaker: KING
text: Ne’er seen but wondered at, and so my state,

speaker: KING
text: Seldom but sumptuous, showed like a feast

speaker: KING
text: And won by rareness such solemnity.

speaker: KING
text: The skipping king, he ambled up and down

speaker: KING
text: With shallow jesters and rash bavin wits,

speaker: KING
text: Soon kindled and soon burnt; carded his state,

speaker: KING
text: Mingled his royalty with cap’ring fools,

speaker: KING
text: Had his great name profanèd with their scorns,

speaker: KING
text: And gave his countenance, against his name,

speaker: KING
text: To laugh at gibing boys and stand the push

speaker: KING
text: Of every beardless vain comparative;

speaker: KING
text: Grew a companion to the common streets,

speaker: KING
text: Enfeoffed himself to popularity,

speaker: KING
text: That, being daily swallowed by men’s eyes,

speaker: KING
text: They surfeited with honey and began

speaker: KING
text: To loathe the taste of sweetness, whereof a little

speaker: KING
text: More than a little is by much too much.

speaker: KING
text: So, when he had occasion to be seen,

speaker: KING
text: He was but as the cuckoo is in June,

speaker: KING
text: Heard, not regarded; seen, but with such eyes

speaker: KING
text: As, sick and blunted with community,

speaker: KING
text: Afford no extraordinary gaze

speaker: KING
text: Such as is bent on sunlike majesty

speaker: KING
text: When it shines seldom in admiring eyes,

speaker: KING
text: But rather drowsed and hung their eyelids down,

speaker: KING
text: Slept in his face, and rendered such aspect

speaker: KING
text: As cloudy men use to their adversaries,

speaker: KING
text: Being with his presence glutted, gorged, and full.

speaker: KING
text: And in that very line, Harry, standest thou,

speaker: KING
text: For thou hast lost thy princely privilege

speaker: KING
text: With vile participation. Not an eye

speaker: KING
text: But is aweary of thy common sight,

speaker: KING
text: Save mine, which hath desired to see thee more,

speaker: KING
text: Which now doth that I would not have it do,

speaker: KING
text: Make blind itself with foolish tenderness.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I shall hereafter, my thrice gracious lord,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Be more myself.

speaker: KING
text: For all the world

speaker: KING
text: As thou art to this hour was Richard then

speaker: KING
text: When I from France set foot at Ravenspurgh,

speaker: KING
text: And even as I was then is Percy now.

speaker: KING
text: Now, by my scepter, and my soul to boot,

speaker: KING
text: He hath more worthy interest to the state

speaker: KING
text: Than thou, the shadow of succession.

speaker: KING
text: For of no right, nor color like to right,

speaker: KING
text: He doth fill fields with harness in the realm,

speaker: KING
text: Turns head against the lion’s armèd jaws,

speaker: KING
text: And, being no more in debt to years than thou,

speaker: KING
text: Leads ancient lords and reverend bishops on

speaker: KING
text: To bloody battles and to bruising arms.

speaker: KING
text: What never-dying honor hath he got

speaker: KING
text: Against renownèd Douglas, whose high deeds,

speaker: KING
text: Whose hot incursions and great name in arms,

speaker: KING
text: Holds from all soldiers chief majority

speaker: KING
text: And military title capital

speaker: KING
text: Through all the kingdoms that acknowledge Christ.

speaker: KING
text: Thrice hath this Hotspur, Mars in swaddling clothes,

speaker: KING
text: This infant warrior, in his enterprises

speaker: KING
text: Discomfited great Douglas, ta’en him once,

speaker: KING
text: Enlargèd him, and made a friend of him,

speaker: KING
text: To fill the mouth of deep defiance up

speaker: KING
text: And shake the peace and safety of our throne.

speaker: KING
text: And what say you to this? Percy, Northumberland,

speaker: KING
text: The Archbishop’s Grace of York, Douglas, Mortimer,

speaker: KING
text: Capitulate against us and are up.

speaker: KING
text: But wherefore do I tell these news to thee?

speaker: KING
text: Why, Harry, do I tell thee of my foes,

speaker: KING
text: Which art my nearest and dearest enemy?

speaker: KING
text: Thou that art like enough, through vassal fear,

speaker: KING
text: Base inclination, and the start of spleen,

speaker: KING
text: To fight against me under Percy’s pay,

speaker: KING
text: To dog his heels, and curtsy at his frowns,

speaker: KING
text: To show how much thou art degenerate.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Do not think so. You shall not find it so.

speaker: PRINCE
text: And God forgive them that so much have swayed

speaker: PRINCE
text: Your Majesty’s good thoughts away from me.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I will redeem all this on Percy’s head,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And, in the closing of some glorious day,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Be bold to tell you that I am your son,

speaker: PRINCE
text: When I will wear a garment all of blood

speaker: PRINCE
text: And stain my favors in a bloody mask,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Which, washed away, shall scour my shame with it.

speaker: PRINCE
text: And that shall be the day, whene’er it lights,

speaker: PRINCE
text: That this same child of honor and renown,

speaker: PRINCE
text: This gallant Hotspur, this all-praisèd knight,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And your unthought-of Harry chance to meet.

speaker: PRINCE
text: For every honor sitting on his helm,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Would they were multitudes, and on my head

speaker: PRINCE
text: My shames redoubled! For the time will come

speaker: PRINCE
text: That I shall make this northern youth exchange

speaker: PRINCE
text: His glorious deeds for my indignities.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Percy is but my factor, good my lord,

speaker: PRINCE
text: To engross up glorious deeds on my behalf.

speaker: PRINCE
text: And I will call him to so strict account

speaker: PRINCE
text: That he shall render every glory up,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Yea, even the slightest worship of his time,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Or I will tear the reckoning from his heart.

speaker: PRINCE
text: This in the name of God I promise here,

speaker: PRINCE
text: The which if He be pleased I shall perform,

speaker: PRINCE
text: I do beseech your Majesty may salve

speaker: PRINCE
text: The long-grown wounds of my intemperance.

speaker: PRINCE
text: If not, the end of life cancels all bands,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And I will die a hundred thousand deaths

speaker: PRINCE
text: Ere break the smallest parcel of this vow.

speaker: KING
text: A hundred thousand rebels die in this.

speaker: KING
text: Thou shalt have charge and sovereign trust herein.

speaker: KING
text: How now, good Blunt? Thy looks are full of speed.

speaker: BLUNT
text: So hath the business that I come to speak of.

speaker: BLUNT
text: Lord Mortimer of Scotland hath sent word

speaker: BLUNT
text: That Douglas and the English rebels met

speaker: BLUNT
text: The eleventh of this month at Shrewsbury.

speaker: BLUNT
text: A mighty and a fearful head they are,

speaker: BLUNT
text: If promises be kept on every hand,

speaker: BLUNT
text: As ever offered foul play in a state.

speaker: KING
text: The Earl of Westmoreland set forth today,

speaker: KING
text: With him my son, Lord John of Lancaster,

speaker: KING
text: For this advertisement is five days old.—

speaker: KING
text: On Wednesday next, Harry, you shall set forward.

speaker: KING
text: On Thursday we ourselves will march. Our meeting

speaker: KING
text: Is Bridgenorth. And, Harry, you shall march

speaker: KING
text: Through Gloucestershire; by which account,

speaker: KING
text: Our business valuèd, some twelve days hence

speaker: KING
text: Our general forces at Bridgenorth shall meet.

speaker: KING
text: Our hands are full of business. Let’s away.

speaker: KING
text: Advantage feeds him fat while men delay.
